name: Istanbul

palette.name: Flag Red
palette.rationale: Turkish national red is highly visible in civic space; it is national-symbol context, not a municipal palette claim.

palette.name: Bosphorus Blue
palette.rationale: Deep blue represents the Bosphorus and the city's water-defined geography.

palette.name: Dome Gold
palette.rationale: Muted gold references mosque interiors, historic ornament, and warm monumental light.

palette.name: Byzantine Brick
palette.rationale: Brick red-brown supports Byzantine walls, cisterns, and layered historic masonry.

palette.name: Tile Turquoise
palette.rationale: Turquoise evokes ceramic and tile traditions without treating them as official city colors.

palette.name: Stone Courtyard
palette.rationale: Warm stone offers a neutral for courtyards, pavements, and historic facades.

palette.name: Tulip Purple
palette.rationale: A restrained purple nods to tulip culture and seasonal floral displays.

palette.name: Ferry Charcoal
palette.rationale: Charcoal adds a modern transport and waterfront-infrastructure layer for interface use.

sources.title: T.C. İstanbul Valiliği - Tarihi ve Turistik Mekânlar
sources.note: Used for historical and touristic context.

sources.title: Gülhane Park
sources.note: Used for garden, rose, and tulip cultural context.

notes: Complete starter record; only Turkish national red is treated as official symbolic context, while city material and cultural layers are editorial.
editorialSummary: Istanbul's complete palette combines national red, Bosphorus blue, dome gold, Byzantine brick, tile turquoise, courtyard stone, tulip purple, and ferry charcoal.
